name: Summarize meeting notes with Google Sheets and Discord

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03491-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Recent from Google Sheets

### Read Recent from Google Sheets

Reads every row from 'Document Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Next: Newest First

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Read Recent from Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Next: Render as HTML

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Discord

### Post Digest to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Next: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Archive Digest in Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
# Summarize meeting notes with Google Sheets and Discord

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize meeting notes with Google Sheets and Discord. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual ocr & extraction is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Recent from Google Sheets → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Discord → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating ocr & extraction in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your ocr & extraction process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
